title: JavaScript Page Redirects Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with redirects and navigation.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript for redirecting a user to a different page?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using the .location.href or .location.replace() properties to navigate programmatically.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using .location.replace() compared to .location.href?
options: It makes your HTML files smaller and more readable.
It 'replaces' the current page in the browser's history, preventing a 'Back Button loop' for the user.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'window.location.href' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
To show the current page's URL and allow you to set a NEW one, which is like the user clicking a link.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' everywhere in your professional JavaScript redirects?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can make the site load much slower and potentially confuse the redirect logic.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for JavaScript page redirects?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using 'window.location.replace()' for login and signup flows to provide a cleaner 'back button' experience.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
